title: CLAUDE.md - Project Context
text: R package implementing EKIO’s visual identity system ggplot2 gt tables.

title: CLAUDE.md - Project Context
text: comprehensive R package provides EKIO-branded themes, color palettes, scale functions, high-level “recipe” chart functions, gt table theming professional data visualizations. Follows EKIO design principles clarity, purposeful color usage, professional presentation standards.

title: CLAUDE.md - Project Context
text: colors.R — Color scale vectors (ekio_blue, ekio_gray, ekio_teal, ekio_orange, ekio_accent), internal sequential/diverging palette lists, ekio_pal() palette accessor, list_ekio_palettes(), show_ekio_palette(), show_all_ekio_palettes() scales.R — Discrete (scale_color_ekio_d, scale_fill_ekio_d) continuous (scale_color_ekio_c, scale_fill_ekio_c) ggplot2 scale functions British spelling aliases theme.R — theme_ekio() (modular, uses theme_sub_* helpers) theme_ekio_map() spatial maps. Platform-aware font selection via .get_ekio_font() recipes.R — High-level chart builders (ekio_histogram, ekio_lineplot, ekio_scatterplot, ekio_barplot) smart aesthetic detection (static color vs. variable mapping) gt_theme.R — gt_theme_ekio() professional gt table styling data.R — Documentation 6 bundled datasets (Brazilian socioeconomic/agriculture data, global fuels) utils.R — Package-level imports globalVariables suppression

title: CLAUDE.md - Project Context
text: Unified palette access: palette types (categorical, small-group, scientific, sequential, diverging) go ekio_pal(). Sequential/diverging palettes also usable continuous scales Smart aesthetic detection: Recipe functions use rlang::enquo() + internal .detect_aesthetic_type() distinguish missing args, static color strings, variable mappings — auto-selecting appropriate scales Modular themes: theme_ekio() uses ggplot2’s theme_sub_*() helpers (requires ggplot2 >= 3.5.0) Color references: Theme gt functions reference exported color vectors (ekio_blue["700"]) instead hardcoded hex values

title: CLAUDE.md - Project Context
text: Imports: cli, ggplot2 (>= 3.5.0), grDevices, gt, rlang Suggests: dplyr, knitr, rmarkdown, testthat (>= 3.0.0), tibble

title: CLAUDE.md - Project Context
text: Documentation: functions use roxygen2 comments #' Naming: snake_case functions variables (e.g., theme_ekio, ekio_pal) Function structure: Export functions @export, include examples @examples. Use @examplesIf rlang::is_interactive() examples produce plots Testing: Uses testthat framework tests/testthat/. Tests exist colors, scales, themes. tests yet recipes gt_theme Use native R pipe Don’t make pipe chains long (max 5-6 functions, max 15-20 lines) Follow tidyverse guide style Avoid cat, always prefer cli. Don’t verbose, always sparing messages write concise messages

title: CLAUDE.md - Project Context
text: concise Use # ---- Section Name ---- separate sections

title: CLAUDE.md - Project Context
text: making changes, run check() fix errors warnings. Ignore notes Palette Lab Shiny app lives repository (viniciusoike/ekioplot-palette-lab), package. depends ekioplot’s stable exported functions

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
text: theme_ekio() applies EKIO’s visual identity ggplot2 plot. builds theme_minimal() curated typography, spacing, color choices.  grid parameter controls major grid lines drawn:  Use theme_ekio_map() spatial visualizations — removes axes repositions legend.
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point(color = ekio_blue["700"], size = 2.5) +   labs(     title = "Fuel Efficiency vs. Weight",     subtitle = "Motor Trend Car Road Tests (1974)",     x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",     y = "Miles per Gallon"   ) +   theme_ekio() ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point(color = ekio_blue["700"]) +   theme_ekio(grid = "xy")

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
text: ekioplot ships ~30 palettes across five categories. Use list_ekio_palettes() explore : Access palette ekio_pal():
code: str(list_ekio_palettes()) #> List of 5 #>  $ categorical: chr [1:7] "cool" "minimal" "contrast" "full" ... #>  $ small_group: chr [1:6] "duo_warm" "duo_cool" "trio_bold" "trio_cool" ... #>  $ scientific : chr [1:4] "okabe_ito" "viridis" "inferno" "plasma" #>  $ sequential : chr [1:8] "blue" "teal" "gray" "orange" ... #>  $ diverging  : chr [1:3] "blue_orange" "blue_red" "teal_orange" ekio_pal("contrast") #> [1] "#1E3A5F" "#DD6B20" "#2C7A7B" "#D69E2E" "#805AD5" "#C53030" ekio_pal("blue", n = 5) #> [1] "#EEF5FA" "#D4E8F5" "#A8D0E8" "#7EB6D8" "#4A90C2"

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
text: Categorical: contrast, cool, minimal, full, muted, binary, political Small-group: duo_warm, duo_cool, trio_bold, trio_cool, quad_earth, quad_vivid Scientific: okabe_ito, viridis, inferno, plasma Sequential: blue, teal, gray, orange, purple, red, green, amber Diverging: blue_orange, blue_red, teal_orange Visualize palette show_ekio_palette():
code: show_ekio_palette("contrast")

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
text: ekioplot provides ggplot2 scales discrete continuous data.

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_color_ekio_d("contrast") +   labs(color = "Cylinders") +   theme_ekio(grid = "xy")

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
text: Sequential diverging palettes work continuous data:  Fill variants available scale_fill_ekio_d() scale_fill_ekio_c().
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = hp)) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_color_ekio_c("blue") +   labs(color = "Horsepower") +   theme_ekio(grid = "xy")

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
text: Recipe functions high-level wrappers create complete, publication-ready plots smart defaults.

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
code: ekio_histogram(mtcars, mpg)

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
code: cyl_counts <- as.data.frame(table(cyl = mtcars$cyl)) names(cyl_counts)[2] <- "n" ekio_barplot(cyl_counts, cyl, n)

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
code: ekio_scatterplot(mtcars, wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
code: data(fuels) world_fuels <- fuels[fuels$entity == "World" & fuels$year >= 1950, ] ekio_areaplot(world_fuels, year, consumption_gwh, fill = fuel)

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
text: Recipe functions automatically detect whether color/fill argument : Missing — uses EKIO blue default color string (e.g., "steelblue") — uses color directly variable — maps applies appropriate EKIO scale
code: ekio_histogram(mtcars, mpg, fill = "coral")

title: Getting Started with ekioplot
text: Four named color scales exported direct use: ekio_blue, ekio_gray, ekio_teal, ekio_orange. provides 10 shades "50" (lightest) "900" (darkest). Named accent colors available ekio_accent:
code: ekio_blue["700"] #>       700  #> "#1E3A5F" ekio_gray["300"] #>       300  #> "#E2E8F0" ekio_accent #>      blue    orange      teal     amber    purple       red     green      gray  #> "#1E3A5F" "#DD6B20" "#2C7A7B" "#D69E2E" "#805AD5" "#C53030" "#38A169" "#718096"

title: Authors and Citation
text: Oike V (2026). ekioplot: EKIO Visual Identity System R Data Visualization. R package version 0.4.0, https://viniciusoike.github.io/ekioplot/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ekioplot: EKIO Visual Identity System for R Data Visualization},   author = {Vinicius Oike},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.4.0},   url = {https://viniciusoike.github.io/ekioplot/}, }

title: EKIO Visual Identity System for R Data Visualization
text: ekioplot implements EKIO’s visual identity system data visualization R. provides professional ggplot2 theme, curated color palettes scale functions, high-level “recipe” chart builders, matching theme gt tables — following EKIO design principles clarity, purposeful color, professional presentation.
code: library(ekioplot) library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_color_ekio_d("contrast") +   labs(     title = "Fuel Efficiency vs. Weight",     subtitle = "Motor Trend Car Road Tests (1974)",     x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",     y = "Miles per Gallon",     color = "Cylinders"   ) +   theme_ekio()

title: EKIO Visual Identity System for R Data Visualization
text: ekioplot CRAN. Install pre-built binary r-universe: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("ekioplot", repos = "https://viniciusoike.r-universe.dev") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("viniciusoike/ekioplot")

title: EKIO Visual Identity System for R Data Visualization
text: theme_ekio() applies EKIO’s visual identity ggplot2 plot, building theme_minimal() curated typography, spacing, color. grid argument controls major grid lines drawn ("y", "x", "xy", "none"): Use theme_ekio_map() choropleths spatial maps — removes axes repositions legend.
code: theme_ekio(grid = "xy")

title: EKIO Visual Identity System for R Data Visualization
text: ekioplot ships ~30 palettes across five categories, accessible single function:  Categorical: contrast, cool, minimal, full, muted, binary, political Small-group: duo_warm, duo_cool, trio_bold, trio_cool, quad_earth, quad_vivid Scientific: okabe_ito, viridis, inferno, plasma Sequential: blue, teal, gray, orange, purple, red, green, amber Diverging: blue_orange, blue_red, teal_orange Named color vectors (ekio_blue, ekio_gray, ekio_teal, ekio_orange) provide 10 shades ("50" lightest "900" darkest) direct use, plus ekio_accent individual accent colors.
code: list_ekio_palettes()        # explore everything, grouped by type ekio_pal("contrast")        # categorical ekio_pal("blue", n = 5)     # sequential, interpolated to 5 colors show_ekio_palette("contrast")

title: EKIO Visual Identity System for R Data Visualization
text: Discrete continuous scales provided color fill (British-spelling aliases included):
code: # Discrete (categorical palettes) scale_color_ekio_d("contrast") scale_fill_ekio_d("cool")  # Continuous (sequential / diverging palettes) scale_color_ekio_c("blue") scale_fill_ekio_c("blue_orange")

title: EKIO Visual Identity System for R Data Visualization
text: High-level builders create complete, publication-ready plots smart defaults. automatically detect whether color/fill argument missing (uses EKIO blue), color string (used directly), variable (mapped appropriate EKIO scale):
code: ekio_histogram(mtcars, mpg) ekio_histogram(mtcars, mpg, fill = "coral") ekio_scatterplot(mtcars, wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl)) ekio_barplot(cyl_counts, cyl, n) ekio_lineplot(ggplot2::economics, date, unemploy)  world_fuels <- subset(fuels, entity == "World" & year >= 1950) ekio_areaplot(world_fuels, year, consumption_gwh, fill = fuel)

title: EKIO Visual Identity System for R Data Visualization
code: library(gt)  head(mtcars[, 1:5], 8) |>   gt() |>   gt_theme_ekio()

title: EKIO Visual Identity System for R Data Visualization
text: Palette Lab interactive app building comparing palettes across many chart types, color-vision-deficiency simulation contrast checks. runs entirely browser https://viniciusoike.github.io/ekioplot-palette-lab/ source lives separate repository: https://github.com/viniciusoike/ekioplot-palette-lab.

title: EKIO Visual Identity System for R Data Visualization
text: See vignette("getting-started", package = "ekioplot") package website https://viniciusoike.github.io/ekioplot/. EKIO

title: Brazilian Municipal Agricultural Production (2022) — brazil_agriculture
text: comprehensive dataset containing agricultural production data Brazil's three major crops municipality, based IBGE's Municipal Agricultural Production survey (PAM - Produção Agrícola Municipal).

title: Brazilian Municipal Agricultural Production (2022) — brazil_agriculture
code: brazil_agriculture

title: Brazilian Municipal Agricultural Production (2022) — brazil_agriculture
text: tibble 16,689 rows 12 variables: code_muni IBGE municipality code (numeric) name_muni Municipality name (character) name_state State name (character) name_region Region name Portuguese (character) crop Crop name English (character) production_tonnes Production quantity tonnes (numeric) area_harvested_ha Area harvested hectares (numeric) yield Calculated productivity tonnes per hectare (numeric) crop_type Crop cultivation type: annual, semi-perennial (character) crop_category Crop category: grains, industrial (character) crop_importance Economic importance: major (character) production_scale Production scale category (integer)

title: Brazilian Municipal Agricultural Production (2022) — brazil_agriculture
text: IBGE - Instituto Brasileiro de Geografia e Estatística Table 1612: Area, production, yield value agricultural production (PAM - Produção Agrícola Municipal) https://www.ibge.gov.br/

title: Brazilian Municipal Agricultural Production (2022) — brazil_agriculture
text: dataset covers Brazil's three major crops municipality 2022: soybeans, corn, sugarcane. represents municipal-level agricultural production data analysis regional agricultural patterns. Crops included: Soybeans: Brazil's top agricultural export (annual grain crop) Corn: Major grain crop domestic export markets (annual) Sugarcane: Industrial crop sugar ethanol (semi-perennial) Geographic coverage: Brazilian municipalities production data crops across regions.

title: Brazilian Municipal Agricultural Production (2022) — brazil_agriculture
text: IBGE. (2023). Produção Agrícola Municipal - PAM 2022. Rio de Janeiro: IBGE.

title: Brazilian Municipal Agricultural Production (2022) — brazil_agriculture
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Load the dataset data(brazil_agriculture)  # View top soybean producing municipalities brazil_agriculture |>   filter(crop == "soybeans") |>   slice_max(production_tonnes, n = 10)  # Check available crops unique(brazil_agriculture$crop)  # View structure str(brazil_agriculture) } # }

what: Brazilian State-Level Agricultural Production Time Series (1974-2023) — brazil_agriculture_states
title: Brazilian State-Level Agricultural Production Time Series (1974-2023) — brazil_agriculture_states
text: time series dataset containing agricultural production data Brazil's major crops state, based IBGE's Municipal Agricultural Production survey (PAM).

title: Brazilian State-Level Agricultural Production Time Series (1974-2023) — brazil_agriculture_states
code: brazil_agriculture_states

title: Brazilian State-Level Agricultural Production Time Series (1974-2023) — brazil_agriculture_states
text: tibble 9,450 rows 8 variables: code_state IBGE state code (numeric) name_state State name (character) year Year observation (1974-2023, numeric) crop Crop name English (character) production_tonnes Production quantity tonnes (numeric) area_harvested_ha Area harvested hectares (numeric) yield_kg_per_ha Productivity kg per hectare (numeric) production_value_brl_k Production value thousands BRL (numeric)

title: Brazilian State-Level Agricultural Production Time Series (1974-2023) — brazil_agriculture_states
text: IBGE - Instituto Brasileiro de Geografia e Estatística Table 1612: Area, production, yield value agricultural production (PAM - Produção Agrícola Municipal) https://www.ibge.gov.br/

title: Brazilian State-Level Agricultural Production Time Series (1974-2023) — brazil_agriculture_states
text: dataset provides state-level time series Brazil's seven important crops 1974 2023, enabling analysis long-term agricultural trends regional specialization patterns. Crops included: Soybeans: Brazil's top agricultural export Corn: Major grain crop domestic export markets Sugarcane: Industrial crop sugar ethanol Cotton: Key fiber crop export commodity Rice: Important food security crop Wheat: Food grain crop, mainly southern states Beans: Traditional protein source food security crop Time coverage: Nearly 50 years data (1974-2023) providing comprehensive historical perspective Brazilian agricultural development.

title: Brazilian State-Level Agricultural Production Time Series (1974-2023) — brazil_agriculture_states
text: IBGE. (2023). Produção Agrícola Municipal - PAM 2022. Rio de Janeiro: IBGE.

title: Brazilian State-Level Agricultural Production Time Series (1974-2023) — brazil_agriculture_states
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Load the dataset data(brazil_agriculture_states)  # Soybean production trends by top states (recent years) brazil_agriculture_states |>   filter(crop == "soybeans", year >= 2010, !is.na(production_tonnes)) |>   slice_max(production_tonnes, n = 50) |>   ggplot(aes(year, production_tonnes, color = name_state)) +   geom_line()  # View structure str(brazil_agriculture_states) } # }

City size categories: Metropolis (1M+): 1 million+ inhabitants Large city (500K-1M): 500,000 1 million inhabitants Medium city (200K-500K): 200,000 500,000 inhabitants Small city (100K-200K): 100,000 200,000 inhabitants

title: EKIO Area Plot — ekio_areaplot
text: Professional area plot smart aesthetic detection. Supports stacked filled (proportional) area charts.

title: EKIO Area Plot — ekio_areaplot
code: ekio_areaplot(   data,   x,   y,   fill = NULL,   palette = NULL,   position = "stack",   alpha = 0.8,   add_zero = TRUE,   ... )

title: EKIO Area Plot — ekio_areaplot
text: data data frame x X-axis variable (supports data-masking) y Y-axis variable (supports data-masking) fill Fill aesthetic. color string variable name. palette Character. Palette name variable mappings. position Character. Stacking method: "stack" (default) "fill" proportional areas. alpha Numeric. Fill transparency (default: 0.8). add_zero Logical. Add horizontal line y=0 (default: TRUE). ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::geom_area()

title: EKIO Area Plot — ekio_areaplot
text: ggplot2 object

title: EKIO Area Plot — ekio_areaplot
code: if (FALSE) { # rlang::is_interactive() ekio_areaplot(ggplot2::economics, date, unemploy)  # Stacked area with groups data(fuels) world_fuels <- fuels[fuels$entity == "World" & fuels$year >= 1950, ] ekio_areaplot(world_fuels, year, consumption_gwh, fill = fuel) }

title: EKIO Bar Plot — ekio_barplot
text: data data frame x X-axis variable (supports data-masking) y Y-axis variable (supports data-masking) fill Fill aesthetic. color string variable name. palette Character. Palette name variable mappings. add_zero Logical. Add horizontal line y=0 (default: TRUE) horizontal Logical. Create horizontal bar plot (default: FALSE) bar_width Bar width (default: 0.8) ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::geom_col()

title: EKIO Bar Plot — ekio_barplot

title: EKIO Histogram — ekio_histogram
text: Professional histogram smart aesthetic detection.

title: EKIO Histogram — ekio_histogram
code: ekio_histogram(   data,   x,   fill = NULL,   palette = NULL,   bins = "sturges",   binwidth = NULL,   add_zero = TRUE,   border_color = "white",   ... )

title: EKIO Histogram — ekio_histogram
text: data data frame x Variable plot (supports data-masking) fill Fill aesthetic. color string variable name. NULL uses EKIO blue. palette Character. Palette name variable mappings. bins Binning method: "sturges", "FD", "scott", numeric. binwidth Width bins (overrides bins specified) add_zero Logical. Add horizontal line y=0 (default: TRUE) border_color Color histogram outline (default: "white") ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::geom_histogram()

title: EKIO Histogram — ekio_histogram
text: ggplot2 object

what: IPS Brasil 2025 - Social Progress Index for Brazilian Municipalities — ips_brasil
title: IPS Brasil 2025 - Social Progress Index for Brazilian Municipalities — ips_brasil
text: dataset containing Social Progress Index rankings top 25 populated Brazilian municipalities 2025. IPS Brasil comprehensive index measures social environmental progress across 5,570 Brazilian municipalities using 57 indicators.

title: IPS Brasil 2025 - Social Progress Index for Brazilian Municipalities — ips_brasil
code: ips_brasil

title: IPS Brasil 2025 - Social Progress Index for Brazilian Municipalities — ips_brasil
text: data frame 200 rows (25 municipalities x 8 measures) 8 variables: codigo_ibge IBGE municipality code (numeric) municipio Municipality name (character) uf State abbreviation (character) populacao_2022 Population 2022 (numeric) measure Indicator measured, one 8 key social progress indicators (factor) rank Ranking position among 25 municipalities measure (1-25, numeric) highlight Municipality name one 7 highlighted cities, empty string otherwise (character) is_highlight Factor indicating municipality highlighted (0 1) rank_labels Formatted rank labels showing positions 1, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25 ordinal suffix (character)

title: IPS Brasil 2025 - Social Progress Index for Brazilian Municipalities — ips_brasil
text: IPS Brasil 2025 - Indice de Progresso Social Brasil https://ipsbrasil.org.br/pt IPS Brasil developed Instituto Imazon follows methodology Social Progress Imperative, using 57 indicators across three dimensions: Basic Human Needs, Foundations Wellbeing, Opportunity.

title: IPS Brasil 2025 - Social Progress Index for Brazilian Municipalities — ips_brasil
text: dataset focuses 25 populated Brazilian municipalities includes rankings across 8 key social progress indicators: Indicators included: Social Progress Index: Overall composite score GDP per capita: Economic indicator (2021 data) Water Sanitation: Access basic services Housing Conditions: Quality housing infrastructure Safety: Personal security measures Healthcare Wellbeing: Health system performance Avg. ENEM scores: Educational outcomes (national exam) Share College Educ.: Percentage population higher education Highlighted municipalities (7 cities special focus): dataset highlights 7 specific municipalities comparison purposes: Sao Paulo (SP), Brasilia (DF), Rio de Janeiro (RJ), Belem (PA), Porto Alegre (RS), Fortaleza (CE), Recife (PE). Data transformation: Rankings calculated rank 1 = best performance rank 25 = worst performance among top 25 populated cities. data long format one row per municipality-indicator combination.

title: IPS Brasil 2025 - Social Progress Index for Brazilian Municipalities — ips_brasil
text: Imazon. (2025). Indice de Progresso Social Brasil 2025. https://imazon.org.br/categorias/indice-de-progresso-social/

title: IPS Brasil 2025 - Social Progress Index for Brazilian Municipalities — ips_brasil

title: ekioplot 0.4.0
text: Removed run_palette_lab() bundled Shiny app. Palette Lab now lives repository (https://github.com/viniciusoike/ekioplot-palette-lab) runs entirely browser https://viniciusoike.github.io/ekioplot-palette-lab/. drops 8 app-Suggests (bslib, colorspace, colourpicker, forcats, ggbump, patchwork, shiny, stringr), leaving ekioplot lean visualization package.

title: ekioplot 0.3.1
text: Rewrote README.md match current API (previous version referenced removed font helpers, theme style names, palette names longer exist).

title: ekioplot 0.3.1
text: Added R-CMD-check GitHub Actions workflow covering Windows, macOS, Linux (release, devel, oldrel). Removed stale repository artifacts scratch files.

title: ekioplot 0.3.0
text: Complete API overhaul. Palette system, themes, scales redesigned cleaner, consistent interface.

title: ekioplot 0.3.0
text: Unified palette access ekio_pal() — palette types (categorical, small-group, scientific, sequential, diverging) accessible single function. Added 8 sequential palettes (blue, teal, gray, orange, purple, red, green, amber) 3 diverging palettes (blue_orange, blue_red, teal_orange). Added curated small-group variants: duo_warm, duo_cool, trio_bold, trio_cool, quad_earth, quad_vivid. Added scientific palettes: okabe_ito, viridis, inferno, plasma. list_ekio_palettes() now validates type argument errors invalid types. ekio_orange now 10 shades (50-900), matching color scales.

title: ekioplot 0.3.0
text: theme_ekio() rebuilt using ggplot2’s modular theme_sub_*() helpers (requires ggplot2 >= 3.5.0). Added theme_ekio_map() choropleth spatial visualizations.

title: ekioplot 0.3.0
text: Added continuous scale functions: scale_color_ekio_c() scale_fill_ekio_c() sequential diverging palettes. British spelling aliases (scale_colour_ekio_c(), scale_colour_ekio_d()) included.

title: ekioplot 0.3.0
text: Added high-level chart builders: ekio_histogram(), ekio_lineplot(), ekio_scatterplot(), ekio_barplot(), ekio_areaplot(). Smart aesthetic detection automatically distinguishes static colors variable mappings applies appropriate scales.

title: ekioplot 0.3.0
text: Added gt_theme_ekio() professional gt table styling EKIO branding. Fixed gt_theme_ekio() crashing tables without summary rows row groups.

title: ekioplot 0.3.0
text: Added fuels dataset (global fuel consumption time series). Added brazil_agriculture, brazil_agriculture_states, brazil_gdp, brazil_population datasets. Added ips_brasil dataset (Social Progress Index Brazilian municipalities).

title: ekioplot 0.3.0
text: Removed dplyr dependency. Removed scales Imports (used directly). Removed dead code obsolete prototype files. Added Shiny palette explorer app (inst/shiny-app/). Added test coverage recipe functions gt theme.
